documents.title: Georgia State University public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: Georgia State pairs central Using AI@GSU technology guidance with the DoD-sponsored Collaborative Human-AI Center for trustworthy AI, robotics, edge computing, and education.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Georgia State University's public AI policy posture is cautious and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Collaborative Human-AI Center
bodies.scope: Georgia State pairs central Using AI@GSU technology guidance with the DoD-sponsored Collaborative Human-AI Center for trustworthy AI, robotics, edge computing, and education.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: Georgia State University has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.title: Pounce chatbot and student-success outcome study
initiatives.description: Georgia State Pounce supports summer-melt tasks; public reporting cites a 3 percentage-point improvement in FAFSA filing and registration, and a $7.6M federal grant is studying chatbot effects on student outcomes across partner institutions.
initiatives.significance: Adds source-backed detail to the public AI posture.

investment.funding_source: U.S. Department of Education Postsecondary Student Success Program
investment.description: Federal grant to study chatbot impact on student outcomes.

collaborations.description: Grant-funded study extends chatbot-outcome research across partner institutions.

initiatives.title: GSU AI Assessment Scale syllabus guidance
initiatives.description: Georgia State CETLOE provides AI teaching resources and a suggested syllabus statement that varies acceptable AI use by assignment according to the AI Assessment Scale.
initiatives.significance: Adds source-backed detail to the public AI posture.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for GA. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Georgia State University is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: Georgia State pairs central Using AI@GSU technology guidance with the DoD-sponsored Collaborative Human-AI Center for trustworthy AI, robotics, edge computing, and education. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

research_quality.compiled_by: codex / collect-institution skill / Worker 1 gap sweep

Georgia State University Pounce chatbot AI student outcomes FAFSA melt percentage
Georgia State University AI assessment redesign generative AI teaching
Georgia State University high performance computing GPU cluster artificial intelligence
gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
Follow-up search closed student_subgroup_impact and outcomes_or_usage with Pounce public outcome evidence and the $7.6M chatbot-outcomes study.
Follow-up search found ARCTIC HPC clusters but no public GPU count; enterprise generative-AI platform user counts and contract values remain not public.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: student success led.
